vehicle.label: Long-Range Surveillance Wing
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance

parts.label: Center Body Pod
parts.spec: Flying-wing center pod, equipment bay door, wing-spar reinforcement, motor pylon at rear

parts.label: Wing Spar (2-piece)
parts.spec: 20mm CFRP main spar, 2-piece centerjoint, anti-rotation key, 800mm half-span each

parts.label: Wing Rib Set x 12
parts.spec: Wing rib NACA airfoil profile, lightening pockets, leading edge sock, trailing edge taper

parts.label: Elevon Servo Pocket x 2
parts.spec: Elevon servo pocket, MG90S or DS215MG, control horn linkage, removable access cover

parts.label: Motor + Pusher Prop Mount
parts.spec: Rear pusher 2814 BLDC mount, 10x7 folding prop clearance, thrust line +1deg down

parts.label: GPS + Pitot Boom
parts.spec: Nose boom 8mm, GPS at tip, pitot static port forward, BEC airflow shielded

parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide)
parts.spec: 4S 16000mAh Li-ion slide-in bay, CG adjust slot, top access hatch, balance lead window

parts.label: Integration Service Backbone
parts.spec: Wing/fuselage integration service backbone with assembly_datum pads, harness_raceway, service_hatch, connector_keying, anti-rotation wing datum, and visible load-path inspection marks

requirements: Provide structural integrity and aerodynamic profile for the wing
Integrate control surfaces with hinges
Ensure thermal dissipation through EPP foam core
Allow for battery and control surface access
Maintain load path continuity from spar to fuselage

risk.mit: Implement tighter manufacturing tolerances and visual alignment aids

risk.mit: Perform load distribution analysis and optimize fastener layout

Wing spars transfer loads from wingtips to motor pod

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Main spar, ribs, carry-through, fuselage frames; continuous load path

physics_paths.path: Lift force from wing skin to spar, then to carry-through and fuselage frames
Heat from electronics in battery bay transferred to spar via thermal vias
Pitch rotation about spar axis during flight

integration_opportunities.opportunity: Integrate thermal vias between spar and battery bay for heat dissipation
Add additional ribs for increased structural integrity
Incorporate anti-rotation mechanism for precise control

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual inspection for alignment and fit

verification_focus.value: Continuous from spar to fuselage frames
Effective heat dissipation from battery bay

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Servo pockets, control horns, pushrod/linkage channels

physics_paths.path_description: Control surface movement is transmitted via pushrod to elevon servo pocket.
Heat generated by servos dissipates to ambient air through the foam core and CFRP spar.
Maintenance team accesses and services the elevon servo pocket through the removable access cover.

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate additional cooling fins on the servo pocket to enhance heat dissipation.
Optimize pushrod routing to reduce friction and improve control surface response.
Increase accessibility by adding more removable panels or larger access covers.

verification_focus.description: Ensure the total mass of the elevon servo pocket does not exceed the budgeted 150g.
Verify the structural integrity of the servo pocket under expected loads and environmental conditions.
Evaluate the thermal performance of the servo pocket to ensure adequate heat dissipation.

generated_parts.label: Camera Gimbal Bay
generated_parts.spec: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window

requirements: Forward downward camera gimbal bay 70mm deep, 2-axis micro gimbal, optical window
Fixed-wing flying-wing UAV, 2m wingspan, 4+ hour endurance
Non-weapon camera/gimbal sensor pod bay with removable insert and window
Payload bay is labeled non-weapon/display-only with removable mission/sensor module
Battery bay uses slide rail, CG marks, and independent payload/battery removal
Lift, landing, and payload loads trace through continuous structure: spar to carry-through to fuselage frames, with fittings at every load transfer

risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to lack of dedicated cooling channels or heat sinks.
risk.mit: Add heat sinks or cooling channels to improve thermal management.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly if spar socket is not properly aligned with wing spar.
risk.mit: Ensure spar socket is clearly marked and aligns precisely with wing spar datum.

risk.desc: Payload bay may become weaponized if design drifts into operational payload details.
risk.mit: Maintain clear labeling and design focus on non-weaponized display-only payloads.

risk.desc: Battery bay could hinder CG adjustment if battery is not independently removable.
risk.mit: Design battery bay with slide rails and CG marks for easy removal and adjustment.

risk.desc: Structural continuity may be compromised if load path breaks at wing root.
risk.mit: Ensure continuous load path from spar to fuselage frames with proper fittings.

manufacturing_strategy.support: orientation: vertical, inspection: visual and tactile

verification_focus.check: temperature sensors show within operational range
